prompt: Build a software application called 'SentinelShield'. This platform is an affordable, all-in-one cybersecurity solution tailored for midsize businesses lacking dedicated security teams. Its core purpose is to detect, prevent, and respond to prevalent cyber threats such as ransomware, phishing, and malware, simplifying complex security tasks through an intuitive interface. It aims to significantly improve the security posture of these businesses without requiring specialized IT security staff.The application should be a web-based SaaS platform accessible to business administrators and employees.Key Features:1. Automated Threat Detection & Alerting: Implement real-time monitoring capabilities for network anomalies, endpoint activities, and email traffic. The system should use predefined rules and potentially machine learning models to identify suspicious patterns indicative of malware, ransomware, or phishing attempts. Upon detection, it must generate immediate, actionable alerts (email, in-app notifications) to administrators, detailing the threat and its severity.2. Managed Phishing Simulation & Training: Provide a module for administrators to schedule and launch simulated phishing campaigns targeting employees. The system should track employee responses (e.g., clicks, credential entry). Based on results, it should assign and deliver interactive, short-form security awareness training modules (e.g., videos, quizzes) on topics like phishing identification, strong passwords, and data handling.3. Endpoint Protection & Vulnerability Scanning: Offer a lightweight agent to be installed on company desktops, laptops, and servers. This agent should provide basic endpoint protection (e.g., file integrity monitoring, suspicious process detection) and report system vulnerabilities (e.g., unpatched software, misconfigurations) back to the central platform. The platform should then provide prioritized recommendations for patching and remediation.4. Incident Response Playbooks & Guidance: For identified threats or security incidents, the system should automatically suggest and guide administrators through predefined incident response playbooks. These playbooks will offer step-by-step instructions, automated actions (e.g., isolating a compromised endpoint), and communication templates for various scenarios (e.g., ransomware, data breach, phishing success).5. Centralized Security Dashboard: A comprehensive, customizable dashboard providing a single view of the organization's security posture. It should display key metrics such as active threats, vulnerability scores, employee training completion rates, incident status, and overall security health, with drill-down capabilities for more detail.Data Model:Entities: - `Organization`: id, name, subscription_plan. - `User`: id, organization_id (FK), email, role (e.g., 'Admin', 'Employee'), status. - `Endpoint`: id, organization_id (FK), device_name, type, OS, agent_version, last_seen, status (e.g., 'Online', 'Compromised'), vulnerabilities (JSONB array of identified issues). - `ThreatEvent`: id, organization_id (FK), endpoint_id (FK, nullable), type (e.g., 'Malware', 'PhishingAttempt', 'Ransomware'), severity, description, timestamp, status ('Detected', 'Investigating', 'Remediated'), associated_incident_id (FK). - `Incident`: id, organization_id (FK), name, description, status ('Open', 'Closed'), severity, start_time, end_time, assigned_user_id (FK), playbook_id (FK). - `Playbook`: id, name, description, steps (JSONB array of actions/instructions). - `PhishingCampaign`: id, organization_id (FK), name, launch_date, end_date, target_users (array of user_ids), status, metrics (JSONB: click_rate, compromise_rate). - `TrainingModule`: id, name, description, content_url, difficulty. - `EmployeeTrainingRecord`: id, user_id (FK), module_id (FK), completion_status, completion_date, score.UI Behavior:The web interface will feature a clear navigation for 'Dashboard', 'Threats & Incidents', 'Endpoints', 'Phishing & Training', and 'Settings'. The Dashboard will present executive summaries and critical alerts. The 'Threats & Incidents' section will list all security events, allowing filtering, searching, and detailed viewing of each incident, including associated playbooks. 'Endpoints' will show a list of all managed devices with their security status and vulnerabilities. 'Phishing & Training' will enable campaign creation, reporting on campaign effectiveness, and management of training assignments. Employee users will have a simplified portal to access assigned training modules and report suspicious emails. The UI must be intuitive, responsive, and provide clear guidance for non-security experts.

features: Automated Threat Detection & Alerting: Real-time monitoring of network traffic, endpoints, and email for suspicious activities, malware, and phishing attempts, with immediate alerts.
Managed Phishing Simulation & Training: Conduct regular simulated phishing campaigns and provide interactive training modules to educate employees on identifying and reporting threats.
Endpoint Protection & Vulnerability Scanning: Agent-based protection for company devices (laptops, servers) with automated scanning for known vulnerabilities and patch management recommendations.
Incident Response Playbooks & Guidance: Step-by-step guides and automated actions for common incidents (e.g., ransomware infection, data breach), including communication templates.
Centralized Security Dashboard: A single pane of glass to view security posture, threat landscapes, training progress, and incident status across the organization.
build_spec.tech_stack: Go backend (for performance and concurrency), React/TypeScript frontend, PostgreSQL database, Kubernetes for scalable deployment, Kafka for event processing, Machine Learning for threat detection (e.g., using scikit-learn or similar libraries integrated into Go).

_agent5.problem: Midsize businesses are increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated cyberattacks like ransomware and phishing due to a lack of dedicated security teams and access to affordable, effective security tooling.
_agent5.description: SentinelShield is an affordable, all-in-one cybersecurity platform specifically designed for midsize businesses without dedicated security teams. It provides essential tools to detect, prevent, and respond to common cyber threats like ransomware, phishing, and malware. The platform simplifies complex security tasks into an intuitive interface, offering automated threat intelligence, employee training modules, and incident response guidance, enabling businesses to significantly enhance their security posture without requiring specialized IT security staff.
